facts.text: Leonard and Jack Rosen, brothers from Baltimore, founded Cape Coral in 1957 after flying over the peninsula and spotting the Caloosahatchee River.
Cape Coral’s canals, designed to give every home water access, stretch over 400 miles, making it the city with the most waterways in the world.
Big John, the oversized grocery clerk statue, stands in the Town Center and has become a quirky local landmark since the 1970s.
Mike Greenwell’s Bat‑A‑Ball & Family Fun Park opened in 1992, later renamed Gator Mike’s Family Fun Park in 2019 after a change of ownership.
The Calusa built a canoe canal that crossed Cape Coral from Matlacha Pass to Yellow Fever Creek, a tributary of the Caloosahatchee River.
Cape Coral’s modern history began when the Rosen brothers flew over the peninsula, deciding to develop a planned waterfront community in 1957.
